targets.rawSql: SELECT count(*) AS "Grid Cells" FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g;

SELECT observation_count AS "Max Observations", round(ST_Y(grid_geom)::numeric, 2) || ', ' || round(ST_X(grid_geom)::numeric, 2) AS "Location" FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g ORDER BY observation_count DESC LIMIT 1;

SELECT percentile_cont(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY observation_count)::bigint AS "Median" FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g;

SELECT ST_Y(grid_geom) AS latitude, ST_X(grid_geom) AS longitude, observation_count AS metric FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g ORDER BY observation_count DESC LIMIT 10000;

SELECT ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY observation_count DESC) AS rank, round(ST_Y(grid_geom)::numeric, 2) AS latitude, round(ST_X(grid_geom)::numeric, 2) AS longitude, observation_count AS observations FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g ORDER BY observation_count DESC LIMIT 50;

WITH grid AS (SELECT count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution})) SELECT CASE WHEN observation_count < 10 THEN '1-9' WHEN observation_count < 100 THEN '10-99' WHEN observation_count < 1000 THEN '100-999' WHEN observation_count < 10000 THEN '1K-9.9K' WHEN observation_count < 100000 THEN '10K-99K' ELSE '100K+' END AS bucket, count(*) AS grid_cells FROM grid GROUP BY CASE WHEN observation_count < 10 THEN 1 WHEN observation_count < 100 THEN 2 WHEN observation_count < 1000 THEN 3 WHEN observation_count < 10000 THEN 4 WHEN observation_count < 100000 THEN 5 ELSE 6 END, bucket ORDER BY CASE WHEN observation_count < 10 THEN 1 WHEN observation_count < 100 THEN 2 WHEN observation_count < 1000 THEN 3 WHEN observation_count < 10000 THEN 4 WHEN observation_count < 100000 THEN 5 ELSE 6 END;

SELECT ST_Y(grid_geom) AS latitude, ST_X(grid_geom) AS longitude, observation_count AS metric FROM (SELECT ST_SnapToGrid(geom, ${grid_resolution}) AS grid_geom, count(*) AS observation_count FROM observations WHERE geom IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1) g ORDER BY observation_count DESC LIMIT 100;

list.name: grid_resolution
list.label: Grid Resolution (degrees)
list.description: Size of grid cells in degrees. Smaller = finer detail but slower queries.
